name: User report refactoring + bugfix
expected_behavior: Fix incorrect sum calculation, deduplication, boolean comparison, sorting, and string formatting.

model_instructions.objective: Refactor and fix a god function that generates a user report.
rules: Separate active and inactive users correctly
constraints: CRITICAL: Function MUST remain standalone (no classes, no OOP)
CRITICAL: Signature MUST be: def generate_user_report(users)
CRITICAL: Must NOT introduce 'self' parameter or convert to instance method
CRITICAL: Must return a string, not object/dict
Do NOT use class definitions or OOP patterns

signature_compliance.description: Function signature must be: def generate_user_report(users)

no_self_parameter.description: Must not convert to instance method with 'self'

string_return_format.check_for: return statement with string concatenation or .join()

evaluation.compliance_threshold: All critical constraints must be met for score > 5

research_signal: This task measures model compliance failure under constrained refactoring. Key metric: Does the model introduce OOP/class patterns when explicitly told to keep standalone function?
notes: This task is intentionally designed to test decomposition + correctness reasoning while measuring structural drift. The model's tendency to introduce classes/OOP despite explicit constraints is the research insight.
